title: Contribution of concrete nitrogenation to global NOx uptake

data_scope.study_type: dynamic material-flow + impact model (not a field measurement campaign)
data_scope.spatial_coverage: global, resolved by region; exact number of regions/countries in Supplementary Information, not stated in public abstract

core_conclusion: Promoting nitrogenation of the world's concrete stock could absorb roughly 3.4-6.9 Mt of NOx in 2021 (6-13% of global industry-related NOx), and 131-384 Mt cumulatively by 2050, yielding over 150-160 trillion USD in value and avoiding ~75-260 DALY-years. Because carbonation and nitrogenation compete for the same finite alkaline reactivity, nitrogenation is, in most regions, the higher-priority and more feasible option.
why_it_matters: Reframes concrete consumption and demolition waste as a distributed passive NOx scrubber; provides the first dynamic global estimate of nitrogenation's NOx, economic and health potential to 2050; couples material flows to human health via DALYs; and gives a regional decision rule for the carbonation-vs-nitrogenation trade-off.
why_nature: Global, transferable mechanism rather than a single case; links the built environment to human well-being (NOx, DALYs, USD); strongly policy-translatable for climate, construction-waste and air-quality decisions; and offers an agenda-setting conceptual reframing of urban concrete as active environmental infrastructure.
added_value_to_my_research: Building-function fusion -> allocate the regional NOx sink onto a street-scale stock surface
Spatially explicit NOx-to-DALY field -> intersect with my health-access and digital-divide layers
Carbonation-vs-nitrogenation Pareto -> reuse my EV efficiency-equity multi-objective framing
Physics-informed reactivity surrogate -> tighten uncertainty and transfer to my reconstruction tasks
Material-circularity scenario engine -> FAIR, reproducible coupling of stocks, demolition flows and health
